name: MUSHROOM & GUINNESS PIE
description: Oh my goodness, this take on a pub classic is so so good. It's a hug in a dish! The mushroom is rich and meaty whilst the Guinness adds a dark umami flavour. It's one for those winter nights after a long day in the cold (or any winter warm-up with Mixed Nutty Pea (see page 134)
ingredients: 1 sprig fresh rosemary, plus extra to decorate
2ml tbsp plain flour, plus extra for dusting
1 x 320g block ready-made dairy-free puff pastry
method: Preheat oven to 180°C | Line a baking tray | Large frying pan on a medium heat | 20-22cm deep pie dish | Rolling pin | Small sharp knife | Pastry brush

Quarter the mushrooms and spread them over the tined baking tray. Drizzle with 1 tablespoon of the oil and season lightly and roast in the preheated oven for 10 minutes. When they're ready, remove them from the oven and set aside

Meanwhile, add the remaining 2 tablespoons of oil to the frying pan. Peel and slice the onions, then add them to the pan and season. Add to the pan and cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally until softened. Reduce the heat to low

Remove the leaves from the rosemary and thyme by running your thumb and forefinger from the top to the base of the stems (the leaves should fall off easily) and finely chop, discarding the stalks. Add the pan along with the garlic and cook for 10 more minutes, stirring occasionally

Pour the ale into the pan, bring to a simmer and cook for 10 more minutes until the liquid has reduced by half. Sprinkle over the flour and stir well any lumps in the flour. Add the flour, mustard and soy sauce and stir gently for 15-20 minutes, stirring regularly. Taste and adjust the seasoning with salt and black pepper and mustard or soy sauce if you like. Leave to cool slightly then spoon the mushroom mixture into the pie dish

Roll out the pastry on a surface with flour and roll out the pastry until it is large enough to cover the top of the pie dish. Brush the rim of the dish with water and lay the pastry over the top. Cut off the excess pastry and crimp the edges of the pastry either by pinching it between your finger and thumb all the way round, or by pressing down against the dish with the back of a fork

Melt the dairy-free margarine in the microwave and brush it all over the pastry. Use a sharp knife to cut a little cross in the centre of the pastry to let steam can escape. Transfer to the oven and continue spraying to with the pastry is golden brown, remove and serve hot
